message.content:
<deliberation>
I will evaluate each main GDPR node against the provided policy chunk, considering required cross-references and only flagging deviations where the policy expressly narrows, contradicts, or otherwise diverges from the GDPR text. Omissions are treated as Non-Deviation per the instructions.

REG-086 (Art49(1)) with references REG-071 and REG-078–REG-082:
- Art 49(1) applies only where no adequacy decision (Art 45(3)) and no appropriate safeguards (Art 46). The policy mentions transfers to foreign PwC network companies and specifically the UK but does not claim it can transfer absent adequacy/safeguards or contrary to Art 49(1). It does not assert reliance on any Art 49 derogation nor state that no such conditions are needed. As this is an omission rather than a contradiction, classify as Non-Deviation.

REG-001 (Art6(1)):
- The policy lists consent (a), contract (b), legal obligation (c), and legitimate interests (f) as legal bases and states that for transfers to processors “we do not require a separate legal basis.” GDPR Art 6(1) requires at least one lawful basis for processing; it does not require a “separate” basis for sharing with processors. There is no explicit contradiction or narrowing of the Article’s scope in the policy, and no GDPR quote mandates a separate legal basis for processor disclosure. Non-Deviation.

REG-022 (Art15(2)) with references to Article 46 (REG-078–REG-082):
- The policy chunk does not mention the right to be informed of appropriate safeguards for third-country transfers. This is an omission, not a contradiction, and thus Non-Deviation per rules.

REG-087 (Art49(2)) with reference to Art49(1)(g) (REG-086):
- The policy does not discuss public registers or any transfer from such registers. No statement contradicts Art 49(2). Omission only; Non-Deviation.

REG-031 (Art20(1)) with references to Art6(1) (REG-001) and Art9(2) (REG-009):
- The policy chunk does not address data portability rights at all. No contradictory statement present. Omission; Non-Deviation.

Conclusion: No explicit deviations are identified within the provided policy chunk for the specified Articles when read together with their cross-references.
</deliberation>
